Definition: Unique identifier for the facility,
Format: Constructed as ISO-3 country code + sequential number (e.g. KEN-001, ZAF-003).
Data Collection Guidelines: Assign sequentially within each country. Use the same ID across data collection rounds to enable longitudinal tracking.
Other Metadata: Primary key. Must be unique across the entire register.

Definition: The official or commonly used commercial name of the data centre facility.
Data Collection Guidelines: Use the name as marketed by the operator. Include any location suffix used commercially (e.g. 'Teraco JB1', 'PAIX Kigali KGL-1'). If the facility has no public name, use '[Operator] [City]'.
Other Metadata: Prefer the English-language name where available. Include local-language name in parentheses if commonly used.

Definition: ISO-3 code for country in which the facility is physically located.
Data Collection Guidelines: Use standard ISO-3 country codes from countries.csv.
Other Metadata: Links to the wider DPI mapping project's country list.

Definition: City or town where the facility is located.
Data Collection Guidelines: Use the most commonly referenced city name. For facilities outside city boundaries, use the nearest city and note the actual location in comments.
Other Metadata: Standardise spelling across entries (e.g. always 'Johannesburg' not 'Joburg').

Definition: Latitude and longitude of the facility.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator websites, Google Maps, and facility marketing materials. Many operators publish approximate locations. Code as 0 if unavailable.
Other Metadata: Approximate coordinates are acceptable. Precision to 4 decimal places is sufficient.

Definition: Current operational status of the facility.
Format: Decommissioned; Operational; Planned; Under construction; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for recent news, operator press releases, and industry databases (e.g. Cloudscene, DataCenterMap). Prioritise sources from the last 12 months.
Other Metadata: Status may change rapidly. Always record the date of the source used.

Definition: Year the facility became operational or is expected to become operational.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator websites, press releases, and news articles for opening dates or construction completion announcements.
Other Metadata: For facilities under construction, record the announced target year.

Definition: Primary classification of the data centre by service model.
Format: Colocation/carrier-neutral; Edge; Enterprise; Government; Hyperscale; Research
Data Collection Guidelines: Determine from the facility's marketed services. A facility offering rack space to multiple tenants is colocation. A facility built for a single organisation's internal use is enterprise/captive.
Other Metadata: A single facility may serve multiple functions. Record the primary type. Note secondary functions in comments.

Definition: Key services offered by the facility.
Data Collection Guidelines: List the main service categories: colocation, managed hosting, cloud services, disaster recovery, connectivity/peering, managed security, etc. Separate with semicolons.
Other Metadata: Focus on what is actively marketed or documented. Do not infer services.

Definition: Whether the facility hosts government data or systems.
Format: Designated national / government data centre; Hosts government data alongside commercial; No government data; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for government cloud contracts, public procurement records, and operator client lists.
Other Metadata: Key indicator for sovereign infrastructure mapping. Note which ministries or agencies if identifiable.

Definition: Name of the entity that manages the day-to-day operations of the facility.
Data Collection Guidelines: Identify the operating company, which may differ from the owner. For government facilities, name the specific agency or SOE.
Other Metadata: Record the current operator. If recently changed, note the previous operator in comments. Select id from organisations.csv. If not present create a new row in organisations.csv

Definition: Ultimate parent company or controlling entity, if different from operator.
Data Collection Guidelines: Trace ownership through corporate filings, press releases, and databases like Crunchbase or Bloomberg. For government facilities, name the ministry or authority.
Other Metadata: Important for mapping concentration and foreign influence. Record the highest identifiable parent in the corporate chain. Select id from organisations.csv. If not present create a new row in organisations.csv

Definition: The topmost entity in the ownership chain and the ultimate controlling parent
Data Collection Guidelines: Traced via corporate registries, SEC/stock exchange filings, annual reports, and press releases
Other Metadata: Digital Realty Trust (self)

Definition: Name/s of country where ultimate parent comaniy/ies are registered
Classification of the ownership structure.
Format: Government / SOE; Joint venture (majority domestic); Joint venture (majority foreign); Multilateral / DFI; PPP; Private domestic; Private foreign; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Determine from company registration, press releases, and corporate websites. For joint ventures, identify the majority shareholder.
Other Metadata: This variable is critical for sovereignty analysis. Take care to distinguish between operational control and equity ownership.

Definition: Legal/corporate structure of the ultimate parent or controlling entity
Data Collection Guidelines: Reflects the structure of the ultimate parent, not the operator itself
Other Metadata: Public Listed Company

Definition: Top 5 shareholders with stakes, for publicly listed or large private entities
Format: Semi-colon delimited list of names and percentages
Data Collection Guidelines: Percentages from most recent available filings (2024-2026); empty for entities where data is not publicly available
Other Metadata: Vanguard (13.4%)|BlackRock (10.8%)|State Street (6.3%)

Definition: Percentage of the ultimate parent owned by government entities (direct state shareholding, sovereign wealth funds, state pension funds)
Data Collection Guidelines: Includes indirect government ownership via state-controlled holding companies
Other Metadata: 100% (Government of Egypt)

Definition: Percentage of the entity owned by non-domestic (foreign) entities relative to the country of operation
Data Collection Guidelines: Empty where not publicly disclosed

Definition: Entities with >25% equity stake or effective board/management control
Other Metadata: Digital Realty (55% of Teraco)|Institutional investors (dispersed)

Definition: How control over the facility is exercised by the controlling entities
Other Metadata: Equity|Board Control

Definition: Development Finance Institutions involved in financing or equity investment, including type (equity, debt, guarantee) where known
Data Collection Guidelines: DFI equity investment with board seats may indicate governance influence; debt-only is lower control concern
Other Metadata: IFC|US DFC|BII|Finnfund|AfDB

Definition: Total reported capital investment in the facility in US dollars.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for investment announcements, DFI project databases (IFC, AfDB, US DFC), and financial press.
Other Metadata: Record the most recent total investment figure. Note whether this is for initial build, expansion, or total campus in comments.

Definition: Whether the facility has announced expansion plans.
Format: Active (under construction); No plans; Planned; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for recent press releases, construction permits, and industry news from the last 24 months.
Other Metadata: Indicates market confidence and future capacity trajectory.

Definition: Notable tenants or anchor clients, if publicly known.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for client announcements, press releases, and operator case studies. Do not speculate; only record publicly confirmed tenants.
Other Metadata: Many tenants are confidential. Only record what is publicly documented.

Definition: Is one or more hyperscaler present at the facility
Other Metadata: TRUE; FALSE

Definition: Narrative description of confirmed relationships with major cloud/AI providers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, etc.)
Data Collection Guidelines: Basis for the 6 binary hyperscaler columns (17-22). Includes anchor tenancy, colocation, equity investment, partnership agreements, and cloud interconnects
Other Metadata: Colocation (AWS, Azure, GCP anchor tenants)

Definition: Whether Microsoft or Azure has any confirmed engagement in the data centre (colocation, anchor tenancy, partnership, equity, interconnect)
Data Collection Guidelines: Added in post-processing modification. Detected via contextual keyword matching on 'microsoft' and 'azure' in hyperscaler_relationships, filtering out negated mentions
Other Metadata: Yes

Definition: Whether Amazon Web Services has any confirmed engagement in the data centre
Data Collection Guidelines: Added in post-processing modification. Detected via 'aws' and 'amazon web services' keywords
Other Metadata: Yes

Definition: Whether Google (Google Cloud, GCP, or Google as investor/partner) has any confirmed engagement in the data centre
Data Collection Guidelines: Added in post-processing modification. Includes Google Cloud, GCP, Google as equity investor, Google subsea cable partnerships (e.g. Equiano), and Gemini AI rollout
Other Metadata: Yes

Definition: Whether the facility or its operator is subject to the US CLOUD Act or equivalent foreign data access legislation.
Data Collection Guidelines: Determine from parent company jurisdiction. Any US-incorporated entity or its subsidiaries can be compelled to produce data under the CLOUD Act regardless of data location.
Other Metadata: Derived variable: cross-reference parent_hq_country with ownership_type. If parent is US-based, exposure = 1.

Definition: Level and type of Chinese entity involvement in the facility
Data Collection Guidelines: Ownership = Chinese parent or major shareholder. Construction/Equipment = Huawei/ZTE/Hikvision infrastructure. Investment = Chinese development finance or equity. Multiple = combination
Other Metadata: 2 - Construction/Equipment

Definition: Names of specific Chinese companies involved with the facility
Data Collection Guidelines: Only populated when chinese_involvement is not '0 - None'
Other Metadata: Huawei|ZTE

Definition: Who controls the facility from a sovereignty perspective
Format: African control; Joint African/foreign control; Other foreign control; US control
Other Metadata: African control; US control; Other foreign control; Joint African/foreign control

Definition: How reliable is the control_category assessment
Other Metadata: high; medium; low

Definition: A concise reason for the control_category
Composite indicator of the facility's dependency on foreign technology, ownership, and legal jurisdiction.
Format: High (foreign ownership, proprietary stack, CLOUD Act exposure); Insufficient data; Low (domestic ownership, open source stack, local compliance); Moderate (mixed ownership or stack)
Data Collection Guidelines: Derived variable: calculate from ownership_type, parent_hq_country, open_source_stack, and cloud_act_exposure. Provide the reasoning in comments.
Other Metadata: Analytical variable for policy research. The derivation logic should be documented and applied consistently.

Definition: Whether the facility offers carrier-neutral connectivity.
Format: Fully carrier-neutral; Limited carriers; Single carrier / captive; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Check operator marketing, peering databases (PeeringDB), and IXP membership lists.
Other Metadata: Carrier neutrality is a key differentiator for colocation facilities and affects data sovereignty options.

Definition: Whether the facility has direct or indirect access to submarine cable landing stations.
Format: At or adjacent to cable landing station; Connected via metro fibre; Dark fibre connection to landing station; Landlocked/no submarine cable access; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Cross-reference with submarine cable maps (submarinecablemap.com, afterfibre.nsrc.org) and operator connectivity specifications.
Other Metadata: Important for latency, bandwidth capacity, and international connectivity.

Definition: Whether the facility offers contractual guarantees that data remains within national borders.
Format: No; Partial (some data classes); Unknown; Yes (contractual guarantee)
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator service level agreements, terms of service, and government hosting contracts.
Other Metadata: Central to data sovereignty. Particularly relevant for government and financial sector clients.

Definition: Whether the facility or operator claims compliance with the host country's data protection law.
Format: Certified / audited compliant; No claim; Self-declared compliant; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator compliance pages, regulator registers, and legal notices. Identify the specific national law (e.g. POPIA for South Africa, NDPR for Nigeria).
Other Metadata: Name the specific law in comments. This links to the broader DPI legal framework analysis.

Definition: Information security certifications held by the facility or operator.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator websites and certification body registers for: ISO 27001, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, SOC 1, SOC 2, PCI-DSS, ISO 22301. Verify certification dates are current.
Other Metadata: List all current certifications separated by semicolons. Note expiry dates in comments if available.

Definition: Whether the facility's software stack is primarily open source.
Format: Mixed open source and proprietary; Predominantly open source; Predominantly proprietary; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Infer from virtualisation_platform, cloud_platform, and container_orchestration fields, plus any operator public statements on technology philosophy.
Other Metadata: Open source stacks reduce vendor lock-in and foreign dependency. Key indicator for sovereignty-oriented analysis.

Definition: Total floor space of the facility in square metres.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator websites, press releases, construction announcements. Convert from sq ft if necessary (1 sqm = 10.764 sq ft).
Other Metadata: Often reported for marketing purposes. May refer to total campus, total building, or raised floor area. Note which in comments.

Definition: Total number of racks the facility can accommodate.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator specifications, marketing materials, and construction announcements.
Other Metadata: Sometimes reported as 'cabinet' capacity. Record as reported and note terminology in comments.

Definition: Total IT power capacity in megawatts.
Data Collection Guidelines: Search operator announcements, Tier certification documents, and industry databases. This refers to power available to IT equipment, excluding cooling and other overheads.
Other Metadata: One of the most commercially sensitive variables. Many facilities do not disclose. If only total facility power is available, note this in comments and record as-is.

Definition: Whether an Internet Exchange Point is hosted at or connected to the facility.
Format: IXP connected (not hosted); IXP hosted on-site; No IXP connection; Unknown
Data Collection Guidelines: Check PeeringDB, African IXP Association (Af-IX), and PCH (Packet Clearing House) databases.
Other Metadata: IXP presence is critical for local traffic exchange and reducing dependence on international transit.

Definition: Whether the facility hosts GPU or AI accelerator infrastructure.
Format: No evidence; Planned; Unknown; Yes (confirmed GPU/AI infrastructure)
Data Collection Guidelines: Search for NVIDIA partnerships, AI cloud service announcements, and operator marketing of GPU-as-a-service or AI workloads.
Other Metadata: Key indicator of AI-readiness. Record specific GPU models if available (e.g. NVIDIA A100, H100) in comments.

Definition: Free text field for additional context, caveats, and notes.
Data Collection Guidelines: Use for: proxy data explanations, conflicting source notes, partial data caveats, recent changes, and any other context that aids interpretation.
Other Metadata: This field is essential for data quality. Encourage detailed notes.

Definition: URLs of all sources consulted for this facility entry.
Data Collection Guidelines: Record every source URL consulted, separated by semicolons. Include operator websites, press releases, industry databases, government gazettes, and certification body registers.
Other Metadata: Minimum 2 sources per facility for High confidence. Single-source entries must be flagged as Low confidence.
